findings.quote: Locate where the ice is fracturing as the glacier moves, by sensing tiny stretches in the cable.
findings.note: WORLD_KNOWLEDGE — distributed acoustic/strain sensing on fiber optics detects micro-strain ('tiny stretches'), so B matches the known mechanism; D (laser pulses melting a channel) is a category-error filler. A and C remain substantive, so the passage still adjudicates; blind pick B.

findings.quote: It finally arrived in 1849, several years after the town's first hotels had been built.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: A is literally true and explicitly stated - the pamphlet appeared in 1841, 'three hotels went up in as many years', and the railway 'finally reached them in 1849', so 1849 is indeed several years after the first hotels. It is beaten only by the prompt's 'imply': A merely restates an explicit fact rather than the implication (the ironic reversal in key D). Attractive to a reader who overlooks the 'imply' delimiter; beatable, not a genuine double-key.

findings.quote: It proved to visitors that the spring's much-advertised cures had never worked at all.
findings.note: PARTIALLY — option C is an absolute overclaim ('never worked at all') eliminable on form, and A is a bland stated date-fact unlikely for an 'imply' stem; that narrows to B vs D (both real spa-town decline mechanisms), undecided without the passage. Lean D.

findings.quote: Att varmt vatten är lättare än kallt och därför lägger sig överst i schaktet.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: literally true per the passage - 'Varmt vatten är lättare än kallt och lägger sig överst i det utrymme det fyller'. A true mechanism detail, not the whole-text statement; beaten by the stem's 'som helhet', which makes the thesis (key B: an abandoned mine can be a modest but real heat store) the best overall fit. Attractive but beatable.

findings.quote: I Bergslagen står hundratals gamla gruvor vattenfyllda sedan brytningen upphörde.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: literally true per the passage - 'Runt om i Bergslagen står hundratals gamla gruvor på samma vis, tysta och fulla av vatten.' A true background detail defeated by the 'som helhet' delimiter that makes the thesis (key B) the best whole-text summary. Attractive because factually correct.

findings.quote: kan bära en blygsam men verklig roll i ortens uppvärmning
findings.note: class 6: idiomatic-temperature miss. The fixed collocation is 'spela en roll'; 'bära en roll' is off-idiom. Defensible only as elevated/poetic license echoing the 'magasin' imagery of the final paragraph. native alternative: 'kan spela en blygsam men verklig roll'

findings.quote: Det viktigaste fyndet var att temperaturgivarna gick att placera ända ner till schaktets botten.
findings.note: PARTIALLY — the stem asks for the measurement PATTERN; option D is instrument-placement methodology (not a pattern) and C's 'oavsett djup och uttagstakt' contradicts the premise of a pattern, leaving the differentiated finding A vs the sampling-confound B. Lean A; passage needed to fully decide.

findings.quote: Ett lugnt uttag rör om skikten mindre och låter det varma ytlagret ligga kvar, medan ett hastigt uttag blandar in kallt vatten.
findings.note: WORLD_KNOWLEDGE — thermal stratification (warm water less dense; slow draw preserves layering) makes C physically correct; B is an absolute and D (pump cooling) off-mechanism. Distractor A is a plausible reversed claim a layperson cannot rule out, so passage still grounds it; blind pick C.

findings.quote: Övergivna gruvor framstår som den självklara lösningen på hela uppvärmningsbehovet i Bergslagens orter.
findings.note: PARTIALLY — 'texten som helhet' wants the thesis; C is an overclaim eliminable on form and A/D are narrow true facts rather than the whole-text point, leaving the measured thesis B as the only summary-shaped option. A/D are substantive, so routed to adjudication; lean B.

findings.quote: Djupa schakt där vattnet kan tas ut långsamt och ostört har de bästa förutsättningarna.
findings.note: WORLD_KNOWLEDGE/PARTIALLY — deeper shafts hold more stable stratification and slow draw preserves it, so A is the physically sensible pick; B is its opposite, C an absolute, D an oddly specific iron constraint. Distractors substantive enough that the passage still confirms; lean A.

findings.quote: Den viktigaste lärdomen blev att subventionen räknat per resa blev dyrare än väntat.
findings.note: PARTIALLY — the stem asks what happened to OCCUPANCY; D is a cost finding (off-subject) and A an absolute ('varje... oavsett'), both weak on form; B describes a pricing choice, not an occupancy outcome, leaving the differentiated result C. Lean C; passage needed to confirm.

findings.quote: Att inrikesflyget bör förbjudas för att tvinga fram en övergång till tåg.
findings.note: PARTIALLY — stance question; D (ban domestic flights) is an extreme off-topic distractor eliminable on form, and C is the hedged 'too early to judge after one winter trial' option fitting journalistic framing. A and B are substantive stances, so passage still adjudicates; lean C.

findings.quote: I det öppna kontoret stör ett urskiljbart samtal koncentrationen mer än den jämna trafiken utanför fönstret gör.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: literally true per the passage - 'det är sällan det högsta ljudet som stör mest, utan det urskiljbara. Ett samtal två bord bort drar örat till sig, medan trafiken utanför fönstret snabbt blir en kuliss man glömmer bort.' The stem is 'överensstämmer bäst med texten' with no explicit 'som helhet', so this true supporting detail competes with the thesis key C; C wins only on being the more encompassing summary. A strong reader could cite A and file a complaint - attractive but beatable, not a clear double-key.

findings.quote: Studiens allra viktigaste fynd var att anställda själva kan föra dagbok över sin koncentration.
findings.note: PARTIALLY blind-answerable, not a form-decided leak. D is a category error (the diary is the instrument, not a finding) eliminable without the passage; A is an over-broad universal (alla slags arbetsuppgifter, oavsett hur krävande) that reads as an overclaim by form. That narrows to B (measured, on-topic effect) vs C (a self-selection correlation). B vs C is genuinely undecided without the passage — C is a substantive alternative result a passage could support. Materially better than chance, not decided -> flag.

findings.quote: Små ljudåtgärder kan hjälpa koncentrationen, men effekten är villkorad och beror på arbetets art.
findings.note: DECISIVE re-check of the round-1 kill (sole-hedged-key form leak): the redesign resolves it. Round 1 killed this because A carried alltid...aldrig, leaving C the only hedge among absolutes. The rebalanced A is now Oftast ar det den jamna trafiken... and B carries de flesta, so hedging is distributed and all four options are ~13-14 words (no length tell, no position tell). C can no longer be selected by FORM alone: B is a substantive opposing overclaim (rosa brus as the reliable solution), A a specific disturbance claim, D a procedural claim that is plausibly true — each is passage-dependent. Residual lean to C comes from domain knowledge (noise-masking effects are typically conditional) plus a mild nuance heuristic, not from structure. WORLD_KNOWLEDGE with substantive distractors -> flag, not kill.
